claim: An N-formyl group on M3 molecules suppresss strong binding to the second position of the groove.

result.claim: An N-formyl group on M3 molecules suppresss strong binding to the second position of the groove.
result.reasoning: The initial searches for the exact claim and its components ("N-formyl group", "M3 molecules", "binding groove") did not yield any direct supporting or refuting evidence. The propositions and chunks returned were either too general, discussed different molecules and binding interactions, or did not contain the specific combination of terms from the claim. There is no information in the provided knowledge base about an N-formyl group on M3 molecules suppressing strong binding to the second position of a groove.
